channel.title: ECS on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in ECS on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: 阿里云全栈实战（一）：生态全景图——阿里云到底是什么
item.description: 刚上阿里云的第一周，我彻底迷失在产品命名的海洋里。ECS、SLB、SLS、RDS、OSS、NAS、PAI、ARMS、ACK、FC、CDN、WAF、RAM、KMS、ROS、CloudMonitor、EventBridge、PolarDB、Lindorm、AnalyticDB、MaxCompute、DataWorks、Flink、DashScope、Bailian、OpenSearch……每个控制台页面都连着三个我没听过的产品。文档假定你已经知道所有东西是什么。英文翻译有时直译，有时意译，偶尔还会缺失。这就是我当初希望有人能直接塞给我的指南，免得我搭进去第一个周末，点点控制台，读着那些只解释功能开关却不说明产品用途的翻译文档。

item.title: OpenClaw QuickStart (10): Production Deploy and the Failure Modes Nobody Warns You About
item.description:
The local install gets you to “it works on my machine.” The server install is what makes it survive a kernel update.

This chapter walks through the deploy I actually use on a 2-core 4G ECS box, then the failures I have seen often enough to put in writing.

Choosing your server

item.title: Terraform for AI Agents (4): Compute — ECS, ACK, or Function Compute?
item.description:
The single most important architecture decision in an agent system is where the agent loop process actually runs. There are three good answers on Aliyun, plus a fourth that almost everyone forgets. Picking the wrong one isn’t catastrophic — you can migrate later — but it costs you weeks of unnecessary scaffolding and several thousand RMB a month in idle compute.

This article walks through all four with working Terraform, the cost crossover, and the operational gotchas I keep tripping on.
